title: 🐛 [Bug Report] UI Elements Overlapping / Misaligned on Multiple Pages

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

labels.name: NSOC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to newly opened issues for NSOC'26

body:
## 🐛 [Bug Report] UI Elements Overlapping / Misaligned on Multiple Pages

### 📋 Summary
Several UI components across NexaSphere are visually broken — elements are overlapping each other or appear misaligned, especially on smaller screen sizes or certain browsers. This degrades the user experience and makes the platform look unprofessional.

---

### 🔍 Where It Happens
| Page / Section | Issue Observed |
|---|---|
| Navbar | Logo and nav links overlap on mobile |
| Hero Section | CTA button overlaps with heading text on small screens |
| Cards / Grid | Cards overflow their containers or clip content |
| Footer | Footer links stack incorrectly on tablet view |
| Forms | Input labels overlap with placeholder text |

---

### 📸 Steps to Reproduce
1. Open NexaSphere in a browser (Chrome / Firefox)
2. Resize the window to mobile width (~375px) or use DevTools responsive mode
3. Navigate to the homepage / profile / dashboard
4. Observe overlapping or misaligned UI elements

---

### ✅ Expected Behavior
- All UI elements should be properly spaced and aligned
- Layout should be fully responsive across all screen sizes (320px – 1920px)
- No content should overflow or clip outside its container

---

### ❌ Actual Behavior
- Elements visually overlap each other
- Text/buttons clip outside their parent containers
- Layout breaks on mobile/tablet viewports

---

### 🌎 Environment
| Field | Details |
|---|---|
| Browser | Chrome 124, Firefox 125 |
| OS | Windows 11 / macOS |
| Screen Size | 375px (mobile), 768px (tablet) |
| Device | Desktop + Mobile (emulated) |

---

### 🛠️ Suggested Fix
- Audit all pages using browser DevTools responsive mode
- Fix flexbox / grid layout issues causing overflow
- Add proper `z-index` values to prevent stacking conflicts
- Use `overflow: hidden` or `overflow: auto` where appropriate
- Test with Tailwind responsive breakpoints: `sm`, `md`, `lg`, `xl`
- Ensure all components use relative units (`%`, `rem`) instead of fixed `px` widths where needed

---

### ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No overlapping elements on any page at 375px, 768px, 1024px, and 1440px widths
- [ ] Navbar is fully responsive and functional on mobile
- [ ] All cards, grids, and forms render correctly without overflow
- [ ] Tested on Chrome, Firefox, and Safari
- [ ] No regression on desktop layout

title: 🔔 [Feature Request] Implement a Notifications System

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

labels.name: NSOC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to newly opened issues for NSOC'26

body:
## 🔔 [Feature Request] Implement a Notifications System

### 📋 Overview
NexaSphere currently lacks a real-time notifications system, which makes it difficult for users to stay updated on activity relevant to them — such as new messages, mentions, connection requests, or system alerts. This feature will significantly improve user engagement and platform stickiness.

---

### 🎯 Goals
- Notify users in real-time about important events within the platform
- Provide a clean, accessible notification UI (bell icon with badge count)
- Allow users to mark notifications as read / unread
- Support filtering by notification type

---

### 🧩 Proposed Features

#### 1. Notification Bell Icon (Header/Navbar)
- A bell icon in the top navigation bar
- Displays an unread count badge (e.g., 🔴 3)
- Clicking it opens a dropdown/panel with recent notifications

#### 2. Notification Types
| Type | Trigger |
|---|---|
| 💬 Message | Someone sends you a direct message |
| 👥 Connection | Someone sends/accepts a connection request |
| 📢 Mention | You are @mentioned in a post or comment |
| 🛠️ System | Admin announcements or platform updates |

#### 3. Notification Panel / Page
- List of notifications with timestamp (e.g., "2 minutes ago")
- Unread notifications highlighted
- "Mark all as read" button
- "Clear all" option
- Link to full `/notifications` page for history

#### 4. Real-time Updates
- Use WebSockets or polling (every 30s) to fetch new notifications
- Show a toast/snackbar for incoming notifications when user is active

#### 5. Persistence
- Store notifications in the database with `read` / `unread` status
- Notifications older than 30 days can be auto-archived

---

### 🛠️ Suggested Tech Stack
- **Frontend:** React component for `<NotificationBell />` and `<NotificationPanel />`
- **Backend:** REST API endpoints — `GET /api/notifications`, `PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read`, `DELETE /api/notifications`
- **Real-time:** Socket.io or polling fallback
- **Database:** Add a `notifications` collection/table with fields: `userId`, `type`, `message`, `link`, `isRead`, `createdAt`

---

### 📐 UI Mockup (Rough)
```
🔔 [3]  ← Bell icon in navbar
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  Notifications              │
│─────────────────────────────│
│ 💬 Ayush sent you a message │  2m ago  🔵
│ 👥 Ravi accepted your req   │  1h ago  🔵
│ 📢 You were mentioned       │  3h ago
│─────────────────────────────│
│  Mark all as read | Clear   │
└─────────────────────────────┘
```

---

### ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bell icon visible in navbar with unread badge
- [ ] Dropdown shows latest 10 notifications
- [ ] Clicking a notification marks it as read and navigates to relevant page
- [ ] "Mark all as read" clears the badge count
- [ ] New notifications appear without full page reload
- [ ] Mobile responsive design
- [ ] Notifications persisted in the database

title: fix: hide default cursor overlapping custom cursor

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

body:
### Description
This PR fixes the UX bug in which the default cursor would appear above the custom red circle cursor when hovering over buttons.

### What was changed
* Added global CSS rules in `globals.css` such that: `cursor: none !important` on `button`, `a`, `input`, `select`, and elements with `role="button"`.

Closes #14

title: 🔍 Feature Request: Search & Filter Functionality

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

labels.name: NSOC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to newly opened issues for NSOC'26

body:
🔍 Feature Request: Search & Filter Functionality
Summary
Add a search bar and filter options to NexaSphere to help users quickly find relevant content without having to manually scroll through everything.

Problem / Motivation
Currently, there is no way to search or filter content within the app. As the platform grows and more data/content is added, users will struggle to find what they're looking for, leading to a poor experience.

Proposed Solution
Add a search bar in the navbar or top section of the main content area
Support real-time filtering as the user types (debounced for performance)
Add filter options (e.g., by category, date, tags, or type — depending on the content structure)
Highlight matched keywords in search results
Show a "No results found" state when there are no matches
Expected Behavior
User types in the search bar → results update dynamically
User applies a filter → content is narrowed down accordingly
Filters and search can be used together simultaneously
Clearing the search/filter restores the full content view
Additional Context
Could complement the lazy loading improvement in https://github.com/Ayushh-Sharmaa/NexaSphere/issues/8 — search results should also lazy load images
Consider storing recent searches in localStorage for quick access
Acceptance Criteria

Search bar is visible and accessible on all screen sizes

Real-time filtering works without full page reloads

Filter options are intuitive and clearly labeled

Empty state is handled gracefully

No existing functionality is broken

title: feat: add search & filter functionality (closes #9)

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

body: ## 🔍 Search & Filter Functionality Closes #9 ## ✨ What I Built - Real-time debounced search (300ms) across Activities & Events - Filter pills: All / Activities / Events - Keyword highlighting in search results - "No results found" empty state - Floating search button (bottom-right) - Ctrl+K / Cmd+K keyboard shortcut - Fully responsive on all screen sizes - No existing functionality broken ## 📸 Screenshots ### Search Button on Homepage ### Search Overlay - Empty State ### Search Results with Keyword Highlighting ### Filter Pills Working ### No Results State ### Mobile View ## 📁 Files Modified - `src/App.jsx` — added searchOpen state, Ctrl+K shortcut, floating button, SearchBar component - `src/components/SearchBar.jsx` — new file, full search overlay UI - `src/hooks/useSearch.js` — new file, debounced search + filter logic ## ✅ Testing Done - [x] Real-time filtering works without page reload - [x] Debouncing prevents excessive re-renders - [x] Empty state shows correctly - [x] No results state handled gracefully - [x] Ctrl+K keyboard shortcut works - [x] No console errors - [x] Existing features unaffected - [x] Tested on mobile and desktop

title: [UX]: The red circle cursor reveals the classic mouse cursor on top of it when hovering over the button.

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

body: When navigating the interface, the site has a custom red circle cursor. However, when the user hovers over a button, the default system cursor (the pointer hand) becomes visible and overlaps with the custom red circle, resulting in two stacked cursors.

title: perf: implement lazy loading for below-the-fold images

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

body:
Closes #8

## Summary
Implements native HTML lazy loading (`loading="lazy"`) for all below-the-fold images to improve initial page load performance.

## Changes
- `TeamMemberCard.jsx` — lazy load member card photo
- `TeamSection.jsx` — lazy load member card photos in the home section
- `TeamPage.jsx` — lazy load member card photos on the dedicated team page
- `TeamMemberModal.jsx` — lazy load modal profile photo (only renders on user click)
- `Footer.jsx` — lazy load all three logo images (NexaSphere icon, NexaSphere full, GL Bajaj)
- `ContactPage.jsx` — lazy load GL Bajaj logo in the address card at the bottom

## What was NOT changed
- `HeroSection.jsx` — above the fold, must load eagerly
- `CinematicOpening.jsx` — critical first paint asset
- `Navbar.jsx` — logo is above the fold

## Testing
- ✅ No React warnings or errors
- ✅ Vite build passes (2517 modules transformed)
- ✅ All existing styling and functionality preserved
- ✅ Only relevant files modified

title: [UX] Clicking logo/title should navigate to homepage

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

body:
## Description

Currently, clicking the NexaSphere logo/title does not redirect users back to the homepage.

## Current Behavior

* The logo and title appear clickable
* No navigation occurs when clicked

## Expected Behavior

* Clicking the logo or project title should navigate users back to the homepage

## Suggested Fix

Wrap the logo/title inside a homepage navigation link so users can quickly return to the landing page.

## Why This Matters

This follows common web navigation conventions and improves overall user experience and accessibility.

title: 🚀 [MEGA ISSUE] Full NexaSphere Overhaul — Animations, Section Fixes, Admin Sync, Code Quality & Vercel Deployment

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

body:
## 📋 Overview

This issue tracks a complete audit and overhaul of the NexaSphere codebase — frontend, admin dashboard, and deployment config. After a thorough review of every file and section, the problems below were identified and must be resolved. The goal is a clean, fully deployable build on Vercel with zero broken pages, no extra spacing, working animations, and a properly synced admin dashboard.

---

## 🎬 1. Entrance Animation — Ensure It Always Fires

**Files:** `src/App.jsx`, `src/shared/CinematicOpening.jsx`

### Problems

- The `CinematicOpening` component renders correctly, but the `cinDone` gate that controls rendering everything else (Navbar, sections, effects) can race with the DOM. On fast devices the opening animation appears skipped, or the page loads blank.
- The commented-out `localStorage.setItem('ns_intro_seen', '1')` line means the intro always replays — this is intentional, but undocumented and the surrounding logic is fragile.
- The `PageIn` fade-in (`translateY(16px) scale(.99) → none`) triggers before layout is ready on initial load, causing a flicker.

### Required Fixes

- Ensure `CinematicOpening` always completes and calls `onDone` reliably — add a **~5 s timeout fallback** so a failed animation never leaves the user on a blank screen.
- Add a **loading skeleton/fallback** behind `CinematicOpening` to eliminate the white flash before the intro starts.
- Add a clear comment documenting the intentional "always replay" behaviour.
- Delay the `PageIn` animation trigger until after first paint using `useLayoutEffect` + double-RAF so the enter animation is always visible.

---

## 🗂️ 2. Section Pages — All Must Be Verified Working

### 2a. Activities Section & ActivityDetailPage

**Files:** `src/pages/activities/ActivitiesPage.jsx`, `src/pages/activities/ActivityDetailPage.jsx`

**Problems & Fixes:**

1. **Remove in-page admin controls.** The `+ Add Event` and `Delete this event` buttons were a quick hack using `window.prompt()` for credentials — a severe UX and security anti-pattern. All event management must go through the Admin Dashboard exclusively.
   - Remove `handleAddEvent`, `handleDeleteEvent`, `askAuth`, and all related `busy` state.
   - Remove the `onDelete` prop from `EventCard`.
   - Remove the `<button className="btn btn-primary btn-sm" onClick={handleAddEvent}>` block (lines ~425–428).
   - Remove the delete button inside `EventCard` (lines ~147–191).

2. **Fix the `manualEvents` fetch.** The URL `/api/content/activity-events/${activityKey}` is called on every render with no loading/error UI. Add a proper loading state and graceful fallback to static data when the API is unreachable.

3. **Fix scroll-reveal animations.** `pop-in` / `pop-left` / `pop-right` animations don't fire on the detail page because the `IntersectionObserver` in `App.jsx` relies on `cinDone` and has already run by the time the detail page mounts. **Fix:** add a local `useEffect` IntersectionObserver inside `ActivityDetailPage` (and all other sub-pages) that runs on mount.

---

### 2b. Events Section & EventsPage

**Files:** `src/pages/events/EventsSection.jsx`, `src/pages/events/EventsPage.jsx`

**Problems & Fixes:**

1. **Standardize date display.** `EventsPage` uses `ev.date` while `EventsSection` uses `ev.dateText ?? ev.date`. Standardize to `ev.dateText ?? ev.date` everywhere.

2. **Fix card animations.** `EventsPage` hardcodes the class `fired` on `.pop-left`/`.pop-right` cards, bypassing the IntersectionObserver — cards appear instantly with no animation. Remove the hardcoded `fired` class and let the local IntersectionObserver handle reveals.

3. **Fix mobile cursor feedback.** The "View Details →" badge inherits `cursor: none` from the global custom cursor style. On touch/mobile devices where the custom cursor is disabled, this removes all pointer feedback. Set `cursor: pointer` on the card and let the global cursor CSS override it on desktop.

4. **Add null-check on `onEventClick`.** If the prop is not passed, the page crashes. Guard with `onEventClick?.()`.

---

### 2c. EventDetailPage

**File:** `src/pages/events/EventDetailPage.jsx`

**Problems & Fixes:**

1. **Audit section spacing.** Several hardcoded `paddingTop: '56px'` and `marginBottom: '56px'` values stack with container padding creating double-spacing. Consolidate to a single `--section-gap` token.

2. **Verify all tabs.** Confirm that Overview, Topics, Speakers, Volunteers, and Acknowledgements all render correctly and transition without layout shift.

3. **Style the `closingNote` field.** It renders but has no visual differentiation — add a subtle styled blockquote treatment.

---

### 2d. About Section & AboutPage

**Files:** `src/pages/about/AboutPage.jsx`, `src/pages/about/AboutSection.jsx`

**Problems & Fixes:**

- Verify all animated elements fire on scroll (local IntersectionObserver fix — same as §2a).
- `AboutSection.css` has duplicate selector blocks — clean them up.

---

### 2e. Team Section & TeamPage

**Files:** `src/pages/team/TeamPage.jsx`, `src/pages/team/TeamSection.jsx`

**Problems & Fixes:**

1. **Remove `TeamSection.tsx`.** Both `TeamSection.tsx` and `TeamSection.jsx` exist; the `.tsx` version is unused. Remove it to avoid confusion and bundle bloat.

2. **Resolve TypeScript mismatch.** `TeamMemberModal.tsx` is TypeScript while the rest of the project is JSX — this requires the TypeScript Vite plugin to be configured. Verify it builds without error; if not, convert to `.jsx`.

3. **Fix hover animation reset.** Team member card hover animations don't reset properly on fast mouse-out. Add `onMouseLeave` cleanup.

---

### 2f. Contact Page

**File:** `src/pages/contact/ContactPage.jsx`

**Problems & Fixes:**

1. **Verify map coordinates.** The Google Maps embed uses placeholder coordinates `(27.5706, 77.6779)` — confirm these are the correct GL Bajaj coordinates or update to the accurate embed URL.

2. **Move inline styles.** The `contactBurst` CSS animation is injected via an inline `<style>` tag inside the component. Move it to `ContactPage.css` to prevent repeated injection on re-renders.

3. **Normalize bottom padding.** `paddingBottom: '120px'` combined with `<Footer />` creates excessive whitespace. Normalize.

---

### 2g. Membership & Recruitment Pages

**Files:** `src/pages/membership/MembershipPage.jsx`, `src/pages/recruitment/RecruitmentPage.jsx`

**Problems & Fixes:**

1. **Verify build performance.** Both pages are very large (37 KB and 57 KB respectively). Verify they build and render without timeout, and add `React.lazy` + `Suspense` wrappers in `App.jsx`.

2. **Verify form endpoints.** `/api/...` submission endpoints must work in the deployed Vercel environment or gracefully degrade with a visible error message.

---

## 🗄️ 3. Admin Dashboard — Full Sync & Missing Page

**Directory:** `admin-dashboard/`

### 3a. Missing MembershipResponsesManager Page ⚠️ Critical

**Files:** `admin-dashboard/src/App.jsx` (line 10 & 40), `admin-dashboard/src/pages/` (missing file)

`App.jsx` imports and routes to `MembershipResponsesManager` from `./pages/MembershipResponsesManager` — **this file does not exist**. The admin dashboard will throw a build error and crash entirely.

**Fix:** Create `admin-dashboard/src/pages/MembershipResponsesManager.jsx` with:

- A table/list view of membership form responses fetched via `api.membership.getAll()`
- Loading skeleton while fetching
- Empty state message when no responses exist
- Display columns: Full Name, Email, Roll No., Course, Branch, Groups Interested, Submitted At
- Export to CSV button (client-side, using the existing data array)

---

### 3b. Admin Dashboard ↔ Frontend Sync

**Files:** `admin-dashboard/src/services/api.js`, `src/App.jsx`

**Problems & Fixes:**

1. **Disconnected data sources.** Events created/edited in the admin dashboard are stored in `localStorage` (`ns_db_events`) in offline mode, but the frontend fetches from `/api/content/events`. These two sources are completely disconnected. Document this clearly or implement a shared mock that both sides read from.

2. **Silent offline mode.** `api.membership.getAll()` in offline mode returns `{ responses: [] }`, but `DashboardHome` shows "Applications: 0" with no indication this is mock data. Add a visible **"Offline Mode — connect Google Apps Script for live data"** banner in the dashboard when running without the env var.

3. **Unsafe migration logic.** The migration block at the top of `api.js` (removes old `ns_db_core_team`) runs silently and can delete real data if someone has a 3-member team. Replace the member-count check with a **version key check** to make this migration safe.

---

### 3c. Admin Dashboard — Extra Spacing & Layout

**File:** `admin-dashboard/src/styles/admin.css`

**Problems & Fixes:**

1. Audit all page containers for double-padding (`.page` + child elements both adding `24px+`).
2. The `.stats-grid` on the dashboard uses `gap: 24px` but individual stat cards also add `margin-bottom`, resulting in uneven spacing on mobile. Fix to use gap-only layout.
3. The sidebar collapses on mobile but there is no hamburger/toggle — layout breaks below 768px. **Add a responsive sidebar toggle.**

---

### 3d. Admin Dashboard Vercel Config

**File:** `admin-dashboard/vercel.json`

**Fix:** The current `vercel.json` is minimal (`{}`). Add proper SPA rewrite rules:

```json
{
  "rewrites": [{ "source": "/(.*)", "destination": "/index.html" }]
}
```

Also add `admin-dashboard/` as a separate Vercel project, or document the monorepo deployment strategy in the README.

---

## 🚫 4. Extra Spacing — Global Audit

The following specific spacing issues must be fixed:

| Location | Problem | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| **HeroSection** (`HeroSection.jsx`) | `paddingTop` set via `nh` in `App.jsx` **and** `HeroSection` adds its own top padding — double offset | Remove internal top padding from `HeroSection` |
| **Section Dividers** (`MotionLayer.jsx`) | `<SectionDivider />` adds spacing **and** `.section` has `padding: var(--section-padding)` — 2× intended gap | Remove section padding from the CSS class **or** remove the divider spacer |
| **Page Banner** (Events, About, Team pages) | `marginBottom: '60px'` on banner + `paddingTop: '56px'` on first content block = 116px of whitespace | Standardize to a single `--page-content-gap` variable of `48px` |
| **ActivityDetailPage container** | `paddingTop: '56px'` on content div below hero + hero's own `padding: '60px 0 52px'` bottom = 108px gap | Reduce content `paddingTop` to `32px` |
| **Footer** | Extra `margin-top` inherited from the last `SectionDivider` | Give Footer its own top border and remove the preceding spacer |

---

## 🔧 5. Code Quality & Structure

| Issue | Action |
|---|---|
| **Dead files** | Remove `src/pages/team/TeamSection.tsx` and `src/components/Wipe.jsx` (Wipe is defined inline in `App.jsx`; the file is a duplicate/stub) |
| **TypeScript without tsconfig** | `TeamMemberModal.tsx` and test files use TypeScript but there is no `tsconfig.json` at root (only inside `admin-dashboard/`). Add a root `tsconfig.json` or convert `.tsx` files to `.jsx` |
| **`window.prompt()` usage** | Remove all `window.prompt()` / `window.confirm()` / `window.alert()` calls from `ActivityDetailPage.jsx` (§2a) — these are blocked in some browsers and inaccessible |
| **Image imports in `api.js`** | 12 team member images are imported directly in the service file. Move to a dedicated `teamData.js` in the admin dashboard, or reference the same assets from main `src/` |
| **Inline `<style>` injection** | `GlitchText`, `ScanLine` in `ActivityDetailPage.jsx` each inject `<style>` tags in component render. Move all to `src/styles/animations.css` |
| **`NotFoundPage` in `App.jsx`** | Defined at the bottom of `App.jsx` — move to its own file `src/pages/NotFoundPage.jsx` |
| **No linting config** | No `.eslintrc` or `.prettierrc` found. Add basic configs to enforce consistent code style across the team |

---

## 🌐 6. Vercel Deployment

**Files:** `vercel.json`, `vite.config.js`

1. **Verify root `vercel.json`** is correct:
   ```json
   {
     "buildCommand": "npm run build",
     "outputDirectory": "dist",
     "rewrites": [{ "source": "/(.*)", "destination": "/index.html" }]
   }
   ```

2. **Verify `vite.config.js`** has `base: '/'` set explicitly to avoid asset path issues on Vercel.

3. **Document env vars** in `.env.example`:
   ```
   VITE_API_BASE              # backend URL (empty = offline/localStorage mode)
   VITE_MEMBERSHIP_SCRIPT_URL # Google Apps Script URL for membership form
   VITE_MEMBERSHIP_SECRET     # secret token for Apps Script
   ```

4. **PWA plugin.** Verify `public/sw.js` doesn't cause stale cache issues on Vercel deployments. Consider setting `registerType: 'autoUpdate'` in `vite.config.js`.

5. **Large assets.** `public/assets/logo-full.png` (1.4 MB) and `logo-icon.png` (1.4 MB) are unoptimized. Compress or convert to WebP to stay under Vercel's 50 MB bundle warning.

6. **Remove `googleapis`.** Listed as a production dependency but never imported in the frontend — the Google Sheets integration uses a client-side fetch to Apps Script, not the server SDK. Remove it to reduce bundle overhead.

7. **Remove `socket.io-client`.** Imported in `src/utils/socketClient.js` but that utility is never imported anywhere in the app. Remove or wire it up.

---

## ✅ 7. Acceptance Criteria

A PR addressing this issue must satisfy **all** of the following before merge:

- [ ] `npm run build` completes with zero errors and zero unresolved import warnings
- [ ] Admin dashboard `npm run build` completes with zero errors (no missing `MembershipResponsesManager`)
- [ ] Cinematic entrance animation plays on every page load and never leaves a blank screen
- [ ] All 6 nav sections (Activities, Events, About, Team, Contact, Home) render fully with correct scroll-reveal animations
- [ ] `ActivityDetailPage` for "Insight Session" shows no `+ Add Event` or `Delete` buttons
- [ ] `EventDetailPage` shows all tabs (Overview, Topics, Volunteers, Acknowledgements) with correct content and no layout gaps
- [ ] Admin dashboard `/dashboard/membership` route loads without crash and shows the Membership Responses table
- [ ] No `window.prompt` / `window.alert` / `window.confirm` calls remain in production code
- [ ] No extra whitespace between sections — all gaps consistent with design tokens
- [ ] Deployed preview on Vercel loads all routes without 404 (SPA rewrites working)
- [ ] Lighthouse Performance score ≥ 75 on mobile

---

## 🗺️ Suggested Implementation Order

1. **Fix the build-breaking crash first** → Create `MembershipResponsesManager.jsx` (§3a)
2. Remove Add/Delete event buttons from the frontend (§2a)
3. Fix Vercel deployment config + remove dead dependencies (§6)
4. Fix all section-level spacing (§4)
5. Fix per-page scroll-reveal animations (§2a–2g)
6. Admin dashboard polish (§3b–3d)
7. Code quality cleanup (§5)
8. Entrance animation hardening (§1)
9. Full QA pass against acceptance criteria (§7)

---

## 📎 References

| File | Note |
|---|---|
| `src/App.jsx` | Main routing and animation orchestration |
| `src/pages/activities/ActivityDetailPage.jsx` | Lines 274–440 (Add/Delete event) |
| `admin-dashboard/src/App.jsx` | Line 10 (broken import) |
| `admin-dashboard/src/services/api.js` | Offline mock DB |
| `vercel.json` (root) | Deployment config |
| `package.json` | Unused deps (`googleapis`, `socket.io-client`) |

title: Feature Request: Search & Filter Functionality

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

body:
## 🔍 Feature Request: Search & Filter Functionality

### Summary
Add a search bar and filter options to NexaSphere to help users quickly find relevant content without having to manually scroll through everything.

### Problem / Motivation
Currently, there is no way to search or filter content within the app. As the platform grows and more data/content is added, users will struggle to find what they're looking for, leading to a poor experience.

### Proposed Solution
- Add a **search bar** in the navbar or top section of the main content area
- Support **real-time filtering** as the user types (debounced for performance)
- Add **filter options** (e.g., by category, date, tags, or type — depending on the content structure)
- Highlight matched keywords in search results
- Show a "No results found" state when there are no matches

### Expected Behavior
- User types in the search bar → results update dynamically
- User applies a filter → content is narrowed down accordingly
- Filters and search can be used together simultaneously
- Clearing the search/filter restores the full content view

### Additional Context
- Could complement the lazy loading improvement in #8 — search results should also lazy load images
- Consider storing recent searches in `localStorage` for quick access

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search bar is visible and accessible on all screen sizes
- [ ] Real-time filtering works without full page reloads
- [ ] Filter options are intuitive and clearly labeled
- [ ] Empty state is handled gracefully
- [ ] No existing functionality is broken

title: [Performance]: Implement Lazy Loading for Images to Improve Initial Page Load

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

body: ### Problem description Currently, images across the NexaSphere platform—particularly in the Team sections (`TeamPage.jsx`, `TeamSection.jsx`, `TeamMemberCard.jsx`) and Footer (`Footer.jsx`)—are loaded eagerly when the page initializes. As the community grows and more high-resolution profile pictures and event images are added, eager loading will cause significant performance bottlenecks, increase the initial page load time (LCP), and consume unnecessary bandwidth for mobile users. ### Current behavior Images are rendered using standard `` tags without the `loading` attribute. This forces the browser to download all images on the page immediately, even if they are far below the user's current viewport. ### Expected behavior All below-the-fold images (especially Team Member photos, logos in the footer, and activity/event images) should utilize HTML5 native lazy loading. They should only be downloaded when they are about to enter the user's visible viewport. ### Proposed solution Add the `loading="lazy"` attribute to the `` tags across the application. For above-the-fold critical images (like the main hero logo in `HeroSection.jsx` or the `Navbar.jsx`), we should explicitly keep or set `loading="eager"` (or omit the lazy attribute) to ensure they render immediately. ### Implementation steps 1. Open the project in your code editor and perform a global search for `` tags by adding `loading="lazy"`. **Example:** ```jsx ``` 4. **Note:** Do NOT add `loading="lazy"` to images in `HeroSection.jsx`, `CinematicOpening.jsx`, or `Navbar.jsx`, as those are at the very top of the screen and should load instantly. 5. Run `npm run dev` to verify that the images still load correctly as you scroll down the page. ### Files/components likely affected - `src/pages/team/TeamMemberCard.jsx` - `src/pages/team/TeamPage.jsx` - `src/pages/team/TeamSection.jsx` - `src/pages/team/TeamMemberModal.jsx` - `src/pages/contact/ContactPage.jsx` - `src/shared/Footer.jsx` ### Labels `good first issue`, `performance`, `gssoc`, `hacktoberfest` ### Acceptance criteria - [ ] `loading="lazy"` attribute is added to all below-the-fold images. - [ ] Above-the-fold images (Hero, Navbar, Loader) are excluded from lazy loading to prevent layout shifts. - [ ] The web application builds successfully without any React warnings. - [ ] Network tab in DevTools confirms that team images are only fetched when scrolling down to the Team section.

title: Add Spring Boot API and FastAPI forms service; update frontend to use API endpoints
body:
### Motivation
- Move from a static/frontend-only setup to a multi-service architecture with a Java API and a Python forms backend for structured content and form ingestion.
- Replace the legacy Node/Vercel server and direct Google Apps Script integration with backend services to allow authenticated admin operations and persistent storage.

### Description
- Overhauled `README.md` to document the new architecture, environment variables, and local run instructions for frontend, Java API, and Python forms service.
- Removed the old Node server and serverless config by deleting `api/core-team/apply.cjs` and `server/vercel.json` and replaced form handling with a new FastAPI service in `server-python` (router, Pydantic models, placeholder sheet/supabase services, and requirements).
- Added a Spring Boot service in `server-java` with `pom.xml`, application entrypoint, CORS and security config, JPA models, repositories, controllers (`EventsController`, `CoreTeamController`, `ActivityEventsController`, `AdminAuthController`), services (`AuthService`, `EventService`), and initial `application.properties` seed file.
- Updated frontend to consume the new APIs by changing `src/pages/admin/AdminPage.jsx`, `src/pages/events/EventsPage.jsx`, and `src/pages/team/TeamPage.jsx` to fetch from `/api/...`, added a small `Skeleton` loading component in `src/shared/Skeleton.jsx`, added a skeleton animation keyframe to `src/styles/animations.css`, and cleared `src/data/eventsData.js` in favor of API-driven events.

### Testing
- No automated tests were run as part of this change.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_69f4e904f5988327b1a5c77c36abc334)

title: Introduce Java API and Python Forms service; wire frontend to backend and add skeleton loaders
body:
### Motivation
- Move site away from static-only content toward a full-stack architecture with an API and a forms backend to enable dynamic content management and admin workflows.
- Provide an authenticated admin API and content endpoints so the frontend can fetch events, core-team, and activity data at runtime.
- Add a lightweight Forms service to handle Google Sheets / Supabase submissions from frontend forms.
- Improve UX while data is loading by adding skeleton placeholders and refactoring pages to use the new API.

### Description
- Added a new Java Spring Boot backend under `server-java` with `pom.xml`, `NexaSphereApplication`, `application.properties`, CORS (`CorsConfig`) and security (`SecurityConfig`) configuration, JPA entities (`Event`, `CoreTeamMember`, `ActivityEvent`), repositories, services (`EventService`, `AuthService`), controllers (events, core-team, admin auth, activity-events), and a seed placeholder `data.sql`.
- Added a Python FastAPI forms service under `server-python` with router `routers/forms.py`, Pydantic model `models/forms.py`, stubbed service modules `services/sheets.py` and `services/supabase.py`, `requirements.txt`, `main.py`, and `.env.example` for credentials.
- Updated the frontend to use the API: refactored `src/pages/admin/AdminPage.jsx`, `src/pages/events/EventsPage.jsx`, and `src/pages/team/TeamPage.jsx` to fetch from `/api/...` endpoints and handle loading state; emptied hardcoded `src/data/eventsData.js` in favor of dynamic fetches.
- Added a lightweight `Skeleton` UI component at `src/shared/Skeleton.jsx` and a `skeleton-shimmer` keyframe to `src/styles/animations.css` for loading placeholders, and updated the `README.md` to document the new multi-service architecture (frontend, Java API, Python forms) and local run commands.
- Removed legacy `server/vercel.json` and added minor utility `Sanitizer` and several service/controller placeholders to support future features.

### Testing
- No automated test suite was included or executed as part of this change (no CI tests were run).

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_69f4e904f5988327b1a5c77c36abc334)

title: Add Spring Boot Java backend and FastAPI Python forms service
body:
### Motivation
- Provide a new Java Spring Boot backend to serve content and admin APIs for events, core team and activity events with simple auth and CORS support.
- Add a lightweight Python FastAPI service to accept and forward form submissions to external sheet/Supabase integrations.
- Include sensible defaults and configuration for local development via `application.properties` and an `.env.example` for the Python service.

### Description
- Added a Maven `server-java/pom.xml` and Spring Boot entrypoint `NexaSphereApplication` with `CorsConfig` and `SecurityConfig` to configure CORS and permit all requests by default.
- Implemented JPA `@Entity` models (`Event`, `ActivityEvent`, `CoreTeamMember`), repositories, controllers for public and admin endpoints, and services including `EventService` and `AuthService` for session tokens and admin auth logic.
- Added utility `Sanitizer`, resource files `application.properties` and `data.sql`, and basic seeding/defaults for the Java app.
- Added a new Python FastAPI service under `server-python` with `main.py`, Pydantic models (`FormSubmission`), routers (`/api/forms/*` and `/core-team/apply`), stubbed integration services (`sheets.py`, `supabase.py`), `requirements.txt`, and `.env.example`.

### Testing
- No automated tests were added or run as part of this change.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_69f4e904f5988327b1a5c77c36abc334)

title: Add backend content storage, admin UI, dynamic events and form endpoints
body:
### Motivation
- Provide a simple backend-backed content store for events and manual activity events so site content can be managed without code edits.
- Add an authenticated admin UI and API to create/edit/delete events and manage manual activity events for activities.
- Route form submissions through the backend and support Google Sheets logging with safer payload handling.

### Description
- Add a persistent JSON content store at `server/data/content.json` and implement `readContent`/`writeContent` helpers with `ensureContentFile` in `server/index.js` and a `defaultContent` fallback.
- Implement admin session handling (`/api/admin/login`) and protected CRUD endpoints for events at `/api/admin/events` (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) and manual activity event endpoints at `/api/content/activity-events/:activityKey` (POST) and `/api/content/activity-events/:activityKey/:eventId` (DELETE) with simple core-team credential checks.
- Introduce form routing in the backend via `appendFormToSheet` and `handleForm` and expose `/api/forms/membership`, `/api/forms/recruitment`, and `/api/core-team/apply` to centralize form submissions and Google Sheets appends, with improved sanitization and sheet tab mapping.
- Update server request parsing and helpers: increased JSON body size limit to `512kb`, add sanitizers (`sanitizeEvent`, `toSafeString`, `normalizePhone`), bearer parsing and admin auth middleware, and configurable sheet tab mapping.
- Frontend changes: add `AdminPage` component and route it under `/admin`, fetch dynamic events from `/api/content/events` and pass them to `EventsSection`/`EventsPage`, add manual activity event add/delete flows in `ActivityDetailPage`, and wire membership/recruitment/core-team submission flows to backend form endpoints instead of direct Apps Script calls.
- UI and UX tweaks: responsive layout fixes in `AboutSection`, `MembershipPage`, and `RecruitmentPage`, minor style/layout adjustments in `ContactPage`, and improved KSS detection logic across events lists and detail routing.

### Testing
- No automated tests were executed for this change.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_69f0c7130d548327b9e1327458856e8a)

title: Add admin dashboard, backend content store, and route forms/events through server
body:
### Motivation
- Enable dynamic management of events from the web UI and centralize form submissions instead of relying solely on Google Apps Script front-ends. 
- Provide a lightweight admin login and CRUD API for events so non-developers can update site event content. 
- Surface backend-managed events to the frontend with a graceful fallback to built-in static data. 

### Description
- Implement a file-backed content store (`server/data/content.json`) with helper functions `ensureContentFile`, `readContent`, and `writeContent` and expose `GET /api/content/events`. 
- Add admin authentication (`/api/admin/login`) with in-memory sessions plus protected CRUD routes for events (`/api/admin/events` POST/PUT/DELETE/GET) and server-side sanitization for event payloads. 
- Centralize form handling server-side via `appendFormToSheet` and new endpoints (`/api/forms/membership`, `/api/forms/recruitment`, `/api/core-team/apply`) that validate inputs and forward to Google Sheets. 
- Frontend changes to fetch dynamic events and fall back to local data, add an `AdminPage` SPA component and admin route handling in `App.jsx`, and wire Events components to accept injected `events` props; plus various responsive/UI tweaks in sections (`AboutSection`, `ContactPage`, `MembershipPage`, `RecruitmentPage`) and safer payload sizes on the server (`express.json` limit increased). 

### Testing
- No automated test suite changes were included in this patch and no automated tests were run as part of this rollout.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_69f0c7130d548327b9e1327458856e8a)
